extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the sprint led out by MØRKØV Michael inside 200m, taking his second stage after crosswinds earlier split the peloton.
tactical_patterns: Crosswind splits reduced breakaway gap from 3:45 to chase time, forcing sprinter teams to burn matches mid-stage.
MØRKØV Michael dropped BENNETT Sam at 200m mark, perfectly timed to prevent early launch and counter-jumping.
UAE Team Emirates paced entire stage despite no sprint interest, protecting 45-second GC margin through positioning not tempo.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: two from two in sprint stages shows form peaked for designated opportunities, not random luck
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: delayed by crosswinds and still fighting position late, suggests team protection gaps or poor positioning radar
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: lead-out accuracy at 200m demonstrates deliberate late release prevents premature sprints
rider_signals.DEKKER David: neutral: green jersey via consistency not wins, positioning discipline over raw speed
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: second place shows legs present but lacks final acceleration or positioning to challenge BENNETT
